Title: Specialized metabolism by trichome-enriched Rubisco and fatty acid synthase components

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular stem/leaf trichomes; leaf surface exudates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acylsugars function in plant defense activities
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduced total acylsugar accumulation by 23%, reduced C5 acyl chains, decreased a flavonoid-like metabolite, and reduced re-fixation of metabolic CO2 into acylsugars
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in S. pennellii LA0716; LC-MS metabolite profiling; GC-MS acyl chain analysis; delta13C stable isotope analysis; RT-qPCR trichome-enriched expression; phylogenetic analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ~220-fold higher in isolated stem trichomes than shaved stems; extremely low in shaved stems
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TRV-based VIGS; growth chamber 16-h photoperiod, 24/20°C day/night
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RBCL (Rubisco large subunit, inferred holoenzyme partner)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering enhanced trichome carbon recycling and acylsugar production in solanaceous crops; likely useful with pathway gene stacking for high specialized-metabolite output
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpRBCS1 is a trichome-enriched, evolutionarily distinct RBCS member in S. pennellii. 2. VIGS of SpRBCS1 lowered total acylsugars and C5 acyl chains and reduced a flavonoid-like metabolite. 3. delta13C analysis showed SpRBCS1 supports re-fixation of metabolic CO2 into acylsugars.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologs form a solanaceous trichome-enriched clade distinct from mesophyll 'regular' RBCS genes; implicated in broader trichome specialized metabolism rather than canonical photosynthetic function

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (SDR) classical subfamily; beta-ketoacyl-(acyl-carrier-protein) reductase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: acylsugar acyl-chain biosynthesis / plastid fatty acid synthase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: medium-chain and branched-chain fatty acid elongation module supplying acyl groups for acylsugars
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces beta-ketoacyl-ACP intermediates to beta-hydroxyacyl-ACP during the fatty acid synthase elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for acylsugar acyl chains
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway component for specialized acyl-chain formation; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: acylsugars with medium-chain fatty acyl groups
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acylsugars function in plant defense activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduced medium-chain acyl chains in acylsugars, including 8-methylnonanoate, n-decanoate, and n-dodecanoate, consistent with a role in medium-chain fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in S. pennellii LA0716; GC-MS acyl chain composition analysis; RT-qPCR trichome-enriched expression; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ~110-fold higher in isolated stem trichomes than shaved stems
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TRV-based VIGS; growth chamber 16-h photoperiod, 24/20°C day/night
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Fatty acid synthase complex components including KAS enzymes (pathway association)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modify acylsugar acyl-chain composition and defense traits; may be stacked with ASATs and upstream branched-chain precursor genes for custom acylsugar profiles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing changed acyl-chain profile without significant reduction in total acylsugar; possible pathway compensation by other FAS components
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpKAR1 is a trichome-enriched SDR closely related to bacterial/plant FAS KARs rather than cytosolic specialized-metabolism SDRs. 2. VIGS did not significantly lower total acylsugars but reduced specific medium-chain acyl groups in acylsugars. 3. SpKAR1 therefore functions in acyl-chain elongation supplying specialized acylsugar biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically close to two capsaicinoid-associated reductases, supporting evolutionary relationship between acylsugar and capsaicinoid acyl-chain elongation pathways
